title: PID 1, Signals, Shutdown, Health, Restart, and Resource Constraints · Independent Container Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives unfamiliar application constraints and must transfer pid 1, signals, shutdown, health, restart, and resource constraints into a new bounded process lifecycle and health policy without copying the workshop scenario. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded process lifecycle and health policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.
Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery.
Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 768 MiB. Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources. Competency probe: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits
steps.instruction:

The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-cpu-memory-pids-limits-19ux2o
  - competency: docker-cpu-memory-pids-limits
    artifact: runtime-policy.yaml
    decision: cpu memory pids limits
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: 19ux2o
    change: |
      init: true
      stop_grace_period: 30s
      healthcheck: bounded-readiness-probe
      restart: on-failure:3
      memory: 256m
      pids_limit: 128
    verify: docker inspect training-worker --format json && docker events --since 10m
    failure: Reject an unbounded probe, restart loop as recovery, shell wrapper that swallows signals, or deployment without memory and PID limits.
    repair: Make PID 1 ownership explicit, bound shutdown and health timing, preserve durable work, and test termination plus resource pressure.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A lab queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 640 MiB. Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources. Competency probe: Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Containers receive safe CPU and memory limits by default.
